concept.title: Filtering with DITAVAL and build variants (audience/platform)
concept.shortdesc: DITA supports conditional processing using attributes such as audience and platform. DITA-OT applies filtering using a DITAVAL file referenced by args.filter.

conbody.p: Exclude print-only content for a WEB build:
Exclude novice-only content for an ADMIN build:
conbody.codeblock:
dita -i map.ditamap -f html5 -o out/html5-web --args.filter=build/ditaval/web.ditaval dita -i map.ditamap -f html5 -o out/html5-print --args.filter=build/ditaval/print.ditaval
dita -i map.ditamap -f html5 -o out/html5-admin --args.filter=build/ditaval/audience-admin.ditaval dita -i map.ditamap -f html5 -o out/html5-novice --args.filter=build/ditaval/audience-novice.ditaval

p.b: Best practice: properties files per variant

conbody.p: Store stable settings in properties files:
This prevents "mystery builds" and makes CI reproducible.

p.b: Common variant patterns you'll use in real projects

ul.li: Platform split: web vs print output styles, image sizes, navigation density
Audience split: admin vs end-user content, warnings, extra setup steps
Feature tiers: community vs enterprise (often driven by product or custom props)
Region/language: often via map roots + language settings; DITAVAL can help when content is shared
